name: The Dual Process
description: Fast automatic System 1 vs slow deliberate System 2. Biases arise when System 1 dominates.

primitives.description: Fast, automatic, effortless pattern matching. Uses anchors, availability, representativeness. Source of all cognitive biases.
primitives.behaviorBias: jumps to fast conclusion using available anchors and stereotypes

primitives.description: Slow, effortful, logical reasoning. Can override System 1 but requires energy and motivation.
primitives.behaviorBias: analyzes carefully, compares options, considers consequences

primitives.description: Every option/stimulus gets an emotional tag (good/bad/scary/exciting). Tags bypass analysis. Somatic markers guide decisions.
primitives.behaviorBias: avoids negatively-tagged options, approaches positively-tagged ones, regardless of logic

primitives.description: Losses weighted ~2x gains. Detects potential loss in any situation. Drives loss aversion, sunk cost, status quo bias, endowment effect.
primitives.behaviorBias: avoids losses, clings to current state, rejects risky changes even when rational

primitives.description: Controls whether System 2 engages. Low energy/high stress = System 1 wins. High stakes + calm = System 2 activates.
primitives.behaviorBias: determines processing depth — high effort = deliberate, low effort = automatic

primitives.description: Aligns behavior with perceived group norms. Provides a default when uncertain. Source of conformity, obedience, bystander effect.
primitives.behaviorBias: follows group consensus, defers to authority, diffuses responsibility in crowds
